response: TEMPERATURE SHOULD BE MONITORED STRICTLY TO ENSURE SAFETY STANDARDS ARE MAINTAINED. STABLE SYSTEMS REQUIRE STEADY SUPPLY OF ENERGY SOURCES. STRESS CAN SIGNIFICANTLY AFFECT SYSTEMS THAT ARE NOT DESIGNED TO HANDLE SUCH STRESS. SIMULATIONS SHOULD BE RUN TO TEST SYSTEMS UNDER STRESS CONDITIONS. SUCH TESTS CAN REVEAL SHORTCOMINGS THAT MAY OTHERWISE GO UNNOTICED. SYSTEMS SHOULD BE STRESSED IN A CONTROLLED MANNER TO AVOID UNDESIRABLE CONSEQUENCES. STRENGTH AND STABILITY SHOULD BE THE PRIMARY CONSIDERATIONS. SEVERAL SCENARIOS SHOULD BE SIMULATED TO COVER ALL POSSIBLE SITUATIONS. STRATEGIES SHOULD BE DEVELOPED TO HANDLE ANY STRESSFUL SCENARIOS. SYSTEMS SHOULD BE STRUCTURED TO SUPPORT SUCH STRATEGIES. ROLL OUT THE PLANS ONLY AFTER ALL SIMULATIONS ARE SUCCESSFUL. SYSTEMS SHOULD BE SCANNED FOR ANY POSSIBLE SHORTS OR SYSTEMIC FAILURES.
